DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, MAR-24-2021 HEARING HELD RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT JON FRIED'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES. THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND ORDERS AS FOLLOWS WITH THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES: WITHIN 5 DAYS, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT IS TO SELECT 50 INTERROGATORIES FROM HIS SET OF EXISTING INTERROGATORIES. WITHIN 20 DAYS, DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE TO SERVE COMPLETE AND VERIFIED RESPONSES TO THOSE 50 INTERROGATOTIES ALONG WITH ANY APPROPRIATE OBJECTIONS. THE COURT DOES NOT RULE AT THIS TIME THAT OBJECTIONS HAVE BEEN WAIVED BY DEFENDANTS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS. WITHIN 5 DAYS OF RECEIPT OF THOSE RESPONSES, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT IS TO EMAIL THE JUDGE PRO TEM AND THE OPPOSING SIDE WITH ANY OBJECTIONS TO THE RESPONSES. THE COURT WILL THEN RULE ON THE MATTER AND/OR MAY SET A FURTHER HEARING ON THE MATTER. DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE INSTRUCTED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER ON THIS TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM BY EMAIL SENT TO PETER.VANZANDT@LEWISBRISBOIS.COM (CC'ING THE OPPOSING SIDE). JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER VAN ZANDT; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories
Off Calendar Mar-24-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant JON FRIED (Tel: 415-590-0650) in pro per;
Atty. SCOTT A. KUDLER, Esq. of Scott A. Kudler, Attorney at Law, A Professional Corporation (Tel: 805-379-0595) for Defendants/Cross-Complainants Wookey Search Technologies Corporation and Mark Gustavson.

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT JON FRIED's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued by both sides.

The Court vacates its tentative ruling and orders as follows with the agreement of the parties: Within 5 days, Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant is to select 50 interrogatories from his set of existing interrogatories. Within 20 days, Defendants/Cross-Complainants are to serve complete and verified responses to those 50 interrogatoties along with any appropriate objections. The Court does not rule at this time that objections have been waived by Defendants/Cross-Defendants. Within 5 days of receipt of those responses, Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant is to email the Judge Pro Tem and the opposing side with any objections to the responses. The Court will then rule on the matter and/or may set a further hearing on the matter.

Defendants/Cross-Complainants are instructed to prepare and submit a proposed order on this to the Judge Pro Tem by email sent to peter.vanzandt@lewisbrisbois.com (cc'ing the opposing side).

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Continued To Oct-28-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs WOOKEY SEARCH TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION, and MARK GUSTAVSON's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

Defendants Wookey and Gustavson's motion to strike the Amended Complaint is denied. The fraud, concealment, and breach of fiduciary duty claims support the prayer for punitive damages. The conspiracy allegations are proper as the Amended Complaint presently has Doe defendants. Lastly, plaintiff may seek emotional distress damages on the fraud claims. Branch v. Homefed Bank (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 793, 799 ["We agree that in cases of intentional misrepresentation recovery for emotional distress need not be accompanied by physical injury."]; Civil Code sections 1709, 3333.

A response to the amended complaint (not including the negligence claim as to which the demurrer is sustained) must be filed and served not later than August 31, 2020.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E Karnow, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/CEK)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs WOOKEY SEARCH TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION, and MARK GUSTAVSON's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT:

The demurrers to the fraud, concealment, and breach of fiduciary duty causes of action are overruled. "Even under the strict rules of common law pleading, one of the canons was that less particularity is required when the facts lie more in the knowledge of the opposite party ...." Turner v. Milstein (1951) 103 Cal.App.2d 651, 658. Gustavson knows whether he harbored a fraudulent intent. The partnership and minority shareholder allegations support the existence of a fiduciary relationship. See Stephenson v. Dreyer (1997) 16 Cal.4th 1167, 1178 [majority shareholder owes fiduciary duty to minority shareholder]; Amended Complaint 15:9-10.
The demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action is overruled. The Amended Complaint pleads misrepresentations of past or existing facts. Amended Complaint 4:13 [Plaintiff and Gustavson "were partners"]; 5:11 ["WOOKEY had secured at least $4 million in funds"]; 5:16 ["corporate funds were available"].

The demurrer to the negligence cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The claim is either redundant of the negligent misrepresentation claim and therefore superfluous, or an improper claim for negligent breach of contract. See North American Chemical Co. v. Superior Court (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 764, 774.

The demurrer to the wrongful discharge in violation of public policy cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that he was discharged for exercising his rights under Labor Code sections 204 and 1194 by seeking his wages.

Gustavson demurrers to causes of action 5, 7, 9, 10, and 11 on the basis that the claims could only be asserted against employer Wookey. These demurrers are overruled. Paragraph 59 of the Amended Complaint alleges that "MARK GUSTAVSON individually and on behalf of WOOKEY and Plaintiff JON FRIED entered into an oral contract." The court must take this allegation as true and there is a factual issue regarding the parties to the contract and the identity of the plaintiff's employer. Further, Gustavson may be liable on the Labor Code wage claims under Labor Code section 558.1(a).

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E Karnow, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/CEK)
